text:
Once upon a time, in a small village near midnight, a sleepy owl sat on a branches owlhoot. He had heard whispers of a mysterious museum. One night, determined to know more, he spread his wings and flew there.

Inside, shelves packed with odd things lined silent halls. A clock owl stared blankly. The owl ventured further. Opposite a dusty elephant stativity, lay velvet.

A mask drifted. Had owlblood spilled?

He pressed a feather. Nothing. He peered.

A thief!

A book! Hidden.

Later, sleeping alone high in branches.

Moonlight.

Stories!

A tale of mice.

He closed eyes.

It felt kind?<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
